message:
style(tests): apply clippy and rustfmt fixes across benchmark and perf test files

Resolve clippy lints and formatting divergences in three test files that
accumulated during recent CI gate and perf-budget work:

- bench_schema.rs: inline format args in error messages (clippy::uninlined_format_args),
  use Rust numeric literal separators for golden-fixture float constants
  (e.g. 41.025_641_025_641_02), replace `.contains(">")` with `.contains('>')` for
  char-based search
- perf_budgets.rs: replace verbose `let-else` with `?` operator for
  `bun_killer_ratio_release_gate_failure`, inline format arg in remediation string,
  fix line-length formatting for `evaluate_required_e2e_ratio_contract`
- e2e_extension_lifecycle_perf.rs: collapse multi-line closures and chained method
  calls into single-line form where rustfmt prefers it, reformat long eprintln macro
  invocation

No behavioral changes — purely mechanical lint and formatting cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
